gamification.title: Quiz
gamification.intro: Question bank aligned with HSK 1 and your course blocks. Coming soon: sessions, scoring, and progress.
gamification.previewHint: Interactive quiz UI will land in the next iteration.
gamification.roadmap: Next: automatic grading, audio (listening), drag-and-drop ordering, and device-local progress.

pinyin.toggleTitle: Use the “Read” tab on the right edge of the page.

settings.panelHint: Your choices are saved on this device.
settings.pinyinDesc: Pinyin column in tables and ruby where global toggles still apply; for each review or grammar line, use the icon next to the hanzi.
settings.translationDesc: Meaning column in tables and, on the dialogues page, under each line; in review mini-dialogues, use the icon next to each line.

home.title1: Course blocks plus extended reference material.
home.blurb: Review, vocabulary, and grammar are linked.
home.modeReviewDesc: Hanzi-first lines with an icon to reveal pinyin and gloss. Read first, memorize later.
home.modeVocabDesc: Full tables per block — Hanzi, pinyin, and gloss side by side.
home.modeVisualsDesc: Illustrated course PDFs: pick a volume and read in the browser.
home.modeGrammarDesc: Structures, notes, and contrasts organized by topic.
home.modeDialoguesDesc: Full conversations by theme. Each review line also has two mini-dialogues below it.
home.modeQuizDesc: HSK 1 question bank (multiple choice, fill-in-the-blank, and more). Interactive sessions coming next.
home.modeTutorDesc: Chat with our AI to practice your Chinese in real time.

index.reviewIntro: Each phrase starts as hanzi only; use the icon beside it to reveal pinyin, then the gloss. Read aloud; then use Vocabulary and Grammar for details.
index.vocabIntro: Hanzi · pinyin · English gloss tables by block. See Grammar for structures and usage.
index.grammarIntro: Structures, notes, and contrasts by block. Isolated words are in Vocabulary.

visuals.title: Visuals
visuals.intro: Illustrated course PDFs. Pick a volume and turn page by page; fullscreen when you want more space.
visuals.empty: No PDFs listed yet. Add .pdf files under web/pdf-content/, then run dev or build again (manifest is generated in prebuild).
visuals.landscapeHint: These sheets work best in landscape. Rotating your phone usually gives a better view.
visuals.landscapeUnsupported: This browser does not allow locking orientation from the page.
visuals.landscapeFail: Could not lock landscape (permission or device policy).
visuals.pdfKeyboardHint: Click the reader area and use ← → to change pages.

blockTitles.22: Demonstratives and place (这 · 那 · 书店)

review.revealPhraseAria: Cycle this line: hanzi only, then hanzi with pinyin, then pinyin plus translation; then back to hanzi only.
review.standalonePhrasesHint: Each line recalls a theme with examples—they are not literary sentences or the only way to say something.
review.standalonePhrasesHintFinal: English summary of what you practiced in blocks 1–14: examples to jog your memory. For Chinese and pinyin, use the other blocks, Vocabulary, or Grammar.

dialogues.pageIntro: Conversations for hearing Chinese in context. On review, model phrases come first (with translation when the toggle is on), then block mini-dialogues.
dialogues.emptyFilter: No dialogues in this category yet. Pick another or “All categories”.

tutor.pageIntro: Practice your Chinese with our artificial intelligence. It will always answer in Chinese to help you learn.
tutor.placeholder: Write anything—even in Chinese if you can!

vocab.noTable: No vocabulary table in this block.

hanziWriter.quizHint: Hint after 2 misses on the same stroke. On the 3rd miss the stroke is accepted and you move on. You can also skip with the button beside.
hanziWriter.replayExitQuiz: Leave quiz and return to animation
hanziWriter.quizDone: Round complete. Replay the animation or practice again.
hanziWriter.unavailable: No stroke animation is available for this character in the current dataset.
hanziWriter.creditsModal: Hanzi Writer library (MIT license). Stroke data lives in the hanzi-writer-data repository; attribution chain includes Make Me a Hanzi and Arphic (1999) sources. Read each repository’s license before redistributing data copies.

grammar.empty: No grammar notes for this block in the source material.

priority.title: Priorities
priority.intro: Each item opens the matching block in the same mode ({mode}).

footer.rest: Hanzi: Noto Sans SC. Table pinyin column: from the course outline.

metadata.siteDescription: Review, vocabulary, and grammar in blocks, from the course outline.
